page: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton3 processor, specifically the Neoverse-V1 model, running at 2.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core testing and software compilation, alongside a top-tier Passmark memory score. Memory bandwidth tests show strong performance for both cached and uncached operations, reaching 6200.54 GB/sec for cached reads and 253.34 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 25% of tested hardware. However, single-core CPU benchmarks and small-model text generation show average to weak performance relative to the hardware class.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the custom ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and memory bandwidth without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. This makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads that scale across many cores. Recommended workloads include large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching layers, high-throughput web serving, software compilation pipelines, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the ARM64 AWS Graviton3 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, translating to 8.0 GB per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance, particularly in software compilation, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance remains average. This hardware profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive ARM64 deployments. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 64 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 512 GB of RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server with 64 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 512 GB RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 memory-optimized instance delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and multi-core performance for demanding database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton3 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core compilation performance
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Well-suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and web serving
